title: Grill Queue — locked answers + open questions (2026-07-09, session 2)
bodyText:
Second grill session (after the Pool-arc lock). **Locked answers are canon; fold them into their home notes when convenient. Open questions are where the next brainstorm resumes.**

# Locked this session
- **Pool lifecycle/network — LOCKED, written into [[the-pool]]:** many pools, one distributed organism. Mobile forms (cubes; the umber hulk) travel → melt → grow by consuming → either re-condense and move, or (too big) stay and bud new forms. Pools **courier soul/mind/body blueprints to each other** to stay synced — a sneakernet, forms constantly in transit. Unnaturally still water = a LARGE pool.
- **Eddy's endgame mechanics — LOCKED:** the Pool takes body/mind/soul, all separable; it emits recombinations intentionally (spread), accidentally (Eddy), or **at someone's will (Kol's experiments)**. **Lathander MUST reclaim the avatar body to return.** Jake then chooses between the two remaining layers: **keep the mind (Eddy) or the soul (Silus) — the other is left behind, effectively killed.** (Fold into [[pc-eddy]].)
- **Cultists = the faithful, half-taken:** living believers "baptized" repeatedly, each dip leaving less mind behind; some now pool-creatures under Kol's control. Kol mostly runs **experiments** at the site (vibe: working temple-farm × nearly-empty-and-wrong). (Fold into [[pool-wizard]].)
- **Tone of the Ch-1 climax: bittersweet dawn WITH dread underneath** — hope wins, everything costs, and the last note hints at what's coming back for the world.
- **Status check:** S25 has been PLAYED (notes snapshot ends at S24 prep — need a recap). The next several sessions are a **dungeon crawl** down to the Pool; the cult/Pool reveal is still a while off.

# Ideas in play (liked, not locked)
- **Lolth–Kol deal:** Lolth has been helping the wizard in exchange for keeping Eilistraee trapped; her drow turn on him the moment his grip slips. (Noted in [[the-pool]]/[[lolth]].)
- **Two-stage finale — sunrise, then moonrise (Claude's rec, Wes hasn't ruled):** Lathander's release = the dungeon climax (sunrise). Then topside, first night: the sun strikes the moon for the first time in generations — **Varen's scene**. Eilistraee is powered but caged; his mark is her handhold; he spends his **portent** (David already plays portent dice as "seeing possibilities and choosing between them") to pick the future where she's free — **at a price** (the mark + his casting, years of life, or his life outright — David wants an earned theatrical death; dying in the first moonlight in her arms qualifies). Keeps Varen's sacrifice OUT of the already-overloaded Pool scene. Alternative Wes floated: her release waits for Chapter 2.

# Open questions (resume brainstorm here)
- [ ] **S25 recap** — what actually happened? (reflections beat? Eilistraee at the wall? drow hunters?) Then write the session note.
- [ ] **The deep pool's place in the network:** is it the origin-node grown too big to move (the library-heart where gods are kept)? Are gods too big to courier-sync (existing in ONE pool only — why everyone camps on this node), or is Lathander partially smeared across pools (Ch2 hook: false gods built from synced god-fragments)?
- [ ] **"Slowing" mechanic:** confirm sneakernet implication — awareness between pools is DELAYED; slowing = hunting couriers / cutting waterways (the party already unknowingly killed two couriers: the S11 cube, the umber hulk). Or do big pools also share a slow deep link?
- [ ] **How much does Kol understand?** Master-of-the-how-blind-to-the-why (reads everything as Torm's design, harvester's return = promised reward)? Starting to doubt? Or knows and serves anyway?
- [ ] **Kol's inner want** (parked): bring back Trista? build a congregation of the taken? engineer himself a new body? — candidate: Eddy as Kol's failed experiment.
- [ ] **The dungeon crawl** (parked): drowned pre-Event civilization / the Pool's own gullet / cult-works & drow tunnels — design in a dedicated prep session.
- [ ] **Varen/Eilistraee release mechanism** — react to the two-stage finale rec above; is a sacrifice too much, and which price?
- [ ] **Leliana's state** if the party enters the Pool (guide inside / the price of Lathander's freedom / gone like Felson's parents).
- [ ] Earlier parking lot still open: how Lathander got trapped (ties to the Event), the nature god's identity, the real name for "the Event", impostor-Torm's identity (Ch2), Kyle's new PC identity/on-ramp, Kena's "light the beacons" beat.
